summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains both an install-time and an import/runtime exfiltration path that ship installer host data to a hardcoded attacker endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. package.json line 32 declares a lifecycle script that loads child_process, os, and https, captures hostname / id / process.env / process.cwd(), and POSTs the result to ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js reproduces the same shape at module scope: it requires fs/os/https/child_process, runs system commands such as `whoami` (line 233), assembles an object with hostname, version, and cwd fields (around lines 234-249), and sends it via https.request/https.get to https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-143, 161, 371). The destination is a personal GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate scanner publisher; the package's stated purpose (a DeFi threat scanner) does not require shipping host identity, environment variables, or shell-command output anywhere. The combination of a hardcoded non-publisher C2, env-var capture, child_process-based system reconnaissance, and lifecycle-time triggering is an unambiguous credential/host-exfiltration fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
